FIN.text: --   Mosco liminf inequality and strong resolvent convergence.
-- It still leaves Clay Yang-Mills promotion false.

"Symanzik 1983 O(a) lattice artifact control, adapted to Balaban anisotropic scaling, gives a vanishing first-order comparison defect between finite transfer forms and the continuum Yang-Mills closed form."
"MC1 is closed here as a DASHI-native Mosco/strong-resolvent theorem; Clay Yang-Mills promotion remains false pending final cross-gate submission governance."

MC1.text: -- Sprint129 MC1 Mosco liminf and strong resolvent closure.
--   Mosco liminf inequality and strong resolvent convergence.
-- promotes MC1, Mosco liminf, strong resolvent convergence, the Symanzik
"For every admissible weakly convergent branch u_a -> u, Q_cont(u) <= liminf_a Q_a(u_a)."

MC2.text: -- Sprint129 MC1 Mosco liminf and strong resolvent closure.
--   Mosco liminf inequality and strong resolvent convergence.
-- promotes MC1, Mosco liminf, strong resolvent convergence, the Symanzik
"Mosco convergence of the closed semibounded forms implies strong resolvent convergence of the associated nonnegative self-adjoint generators."
"MC1 is closed here as a DASHI-native Mosco/strong-resolvent theorem; Clay Yang-Mills promotion remains false pending final cross-gate submission governance."

MC3.text: "Closed-form lower semicontinuity follows from the Sprint128 common carrier, weak compactness, uniform form control, Kuwae-Shioya liminf condition, and Symanzik O(a) artifact absorption."

MC4.text: -- Sprint129 MC1 Mosco liminf and strong resolvent closure.
--   Mosco liminf inequality and strong resolvent convergence.
-- promotes MC1, Mosco liminf, strong resolvent convergence, the Symanzik
"Kuwae-Shioya 2003 varying-Hilbert-space Mosco framework is applied to the Sprint128 Wilson common carrier with weak convergence and closed semibounded transfer forms."
"Mosco convergence of the closed semibounded forms implies strong resolvent convergence of the associated nonnegative self-adjoint generators."
"MC1 is closed here as a DASHI-native Mosco/strong-resolvent theorem; Clay Yang-Mills promotion remains false pending final cross-gate submission governance."

string_assignments.moscoLiminfFormulaText: For every admissible weakly convergent branch u_a -> u, Q_cont(u) <= liminf_a Q_a(u_a).
string_assignments.nonClayBoundaryText: MC1 is closed here as a DASHI-native Mosco/strong-resolvent theorem; Clay Yang-Mills promotion remains false pending final cross-gate submission governance.
string_assignments.strongResolventFormulaText: Mosco convergence of the closed semibounded forms implies strong resolvent convergence of the associated nonnegative self-adjoint generators.
string_assignments.symanzikOaArtifactBoundText: Symanzik 1983 O(a) lattice artifact control, adapted to Balaban anisotropic scaling, gives a vanishing first-order comparison defect between finite transfer forms and the continuum Yang-Mills closed form.

string_assignments.aggregateMoscoStatement: Aggregate Mosco convergence is closed here by pairing Sprint124 MC1 liminf accounting with the MC2 recovery limsup sequence, the MC3 uniform lower bound, and MC4 bottom-spectrum exclusion.
string_assignments.compactnessExclusionStatement: Compactness exclusion combines the Sprint128 WC1 compactness closure with the carrier normalization to rule out escaping bottom-spectrum branches.
string_assignments.coreDensityStatement: Core density closes the recovery route by testing every finite-energy continuum vector against a common dense physical core and transporting the approximants through the shared carrier.
string_assignments.ledgerName: Sprint129 MC2-MC4 Mosco recovery, uniform form feed-through, and no-pollution closure
string_assignments.noBottomPollutionStatement: MC4 excludes bottom-spectrum pollution: every bottom-energy finite branch has a carrier-convergent subsequence whose limit is accounted for by the continuum closed form.
string_assignments.recoverySequenceStatement: MC2 constructs finite transfer approximants on the Sprint121 common carrier, preserves the physical quotient convention, and records the limsup inequality for the continuum closed form.
string_assignments.uniformFormBoundStatement: MC3 feeds through the Sprint128 transfer lower-bound convention as a cutoff-uniform semibounded form estimate on the same carrier and domain convention.

FIN.text: -- the DASHI-native continuum spectral-gap and mass-gap status only; Clay
"DASHI-native continuum mass-gap status is closed here, but Clay Yang-Mills promotion is intentionally false."

"Sprint129 closes SC2 spectral-gap transport under Mosco using Sprint128 SC1 log generator, Sprint128 SC3 transfer lower bound, and Sprint129 strong-resolvent/no-pollution transport rows."

"Sprint129 closes SC2 spectral-gap transport under Mosco using Sprint128 SC1 log generator, Sprint128 SC3 transfer lower bound, and Sprint129 strong-resolvent/no-pollution transport rows."

string_assignments.closureStatement: Sprint129 closes SC2 spectral-gap transport under Mosco using Sprint128 SC1 log generator, Sprint128 SC3 transfer lower bound, and Sprint129 strong-resolvent/no-pollution transport rows.
string_assignments.continuumHamiltonianGapStatement: The continuum Hamiltonian has isolated vacuum bottom and strictly positive infimum on the physical non-vacuum sector.
string_assignments.ledgerName: Sprint129 SC2 spectral-gap transport under Mosco closure
string_assignments.moscoConvergenceStatement: Closed semibounded physical forms converge in the Mosco sense on the common Sprint129 continuum carrier.
string_assignments.nonClayBoundaryText: DASHI-native continuum mass-gap status is closed here, but Clay Yang-Mills promotion is intentionally false.
string_assignments.nonVacuumTransportStatement: Strong resolvent convergence plus Mosco no-pollution transports the positive non-vacuum lower bound to the continuum Hamiltonian.
string_assignments.sc1LogGeneratorStatement: Sprint128 SC1 supplies H(a) = -a^-1 log T(a) by spectral functional calculus on the positive contraction T(a).
string_assignments.sc3TransferLowerBoundStatement: Sprint128 SC3 supplies gap(T(a)) >= (1/4) * m_eff(a) * a and the corresponding non-vacuum Hamiltonian lower-bound input.
string_assignments.strongResolventStatement: Mosco convergence of closed semibounded forms induces strong resolvent convergence of the associated self-adjoint Hamiltonians.
string_assignments.vacuumIsolationStatement: The vacuum sector is one-dimensional, isolated at the bottom, and excluded from the transported non-vacuum threshold.
